rationale: The citation is not a passing mention: the paper quotes Jain and Srivastava's asymmetry result in the problem setup and repeatedly contrasts its own theory against that benchmark. Jain and Srivastava is also used as part of the experimental foundation that consumers perceive PMG stores as lower priced, which motivates the model. Later, the citing paper references similar simplifying assumptions used by Jain and Srivastava in its heterogeneous-cost extension. These uses indicate a meaningful conceptual role in framing and refining prior conclusions.
relation_description: The citing paper treats Jain and Srivastava (2000) as a core prior benchmark that links PMG low-price signaling to sufficient store asymmetry, then develops a model showing signaling can also arise with homogeneous firms under different consumer information and search assumptions. This qualifies the boundary conditions of the cited paper's conclusion rather than merely listing it.
